title: Alchemilla exigua
intro: Alchemilla exigua is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the lady's mantle or alchemilla. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, menstrual disorders, and inflammatory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Alchemilla exigua include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Alchemilla exigua include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe digestive issues and promote menstrual health.
